exemplar.title:
What is the effect of diluting acetone and denatured alcohol mixtures obtained from
acetone-based nail polish removers through distillation in dissolving nail polish from
artificial nails?

detailedExemplarData.title:
What is the effect of diluting acetone and denatured alcohol mixtures obtained from
acetone-based nail polish removers through distillation in dissolving nail polish from
artificial nails?

strengths: Research question is clearly stated under a dedicated heading and aligns with the investigationâs aim
Topic is communicated accurately and coherently, with purpose and focus maintained throughout
Methodology is outlined in a clear three-part procedure supported by ethical and environmental considerations
Use of artificial nails to control human-subject variability is well justified
weaknesses: Purity of the distillate is assumed rather than verified, limiting methodological rigor
Controlled variable descriptions (e.g., distillation temperature) lack quantitative ranges or calibration details
Minor digressions in background (health-impact discussion) slightly dilute focus
Some connections between method and later discussion are not always tight
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work solidly in the 3â4 band. The research question is well formulated and maintained, and the methodology is generally appropriate, but key controls (purity verification, quantitative variable limits) are missing. Overall performance merits a 4 out of 6.

strengths: Consistent and accurate use of subject-specific terminology (e.g., âhydrogen bondingâ, âdielectric constantâ, âsimple distillationâ)
Relevant source material is cited for background chemistry and safety data
Basic knowledge of polarity, bonding, distillation and nail anatomy is clear and coherent
weaknesses: Source material is mostly descriptive and not fully integrated into data interpretation or evaluation
Chemical explanations (e.g., mass-change as proxy for polish removal) are thin or contain misconceptions
Limited quantitative justification of chemical reasoning (e.g., lack of dielectric constant comparison)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Application of sources and subject knowledge align with the mid-3â4 band, but limited integration into analysis and occasional misconceptions restrain performance. Accurate terminology lifts the criterion but overall it sits at 3 out of 6.

strengths: Primary data collection (five concentrations, five replicates) with uncertainty analysis demonstrates solid research effort
Data are processed (means, standard deviations, error bars) and a clear trend is visualized in graphs
Qualitative observations complement quantitative results and add depth to evaluation
Reflective discussion identifies weaknesses and proposes realistic improvements and extensions
weaknesses: Measurement resolution of the balance limits reliability for lower concentrations
Analysis remains surface-level with limited quantitative chemical justification
Evaluation is not fully criticalâalternative explanations and the impact of drying-time inconsistencies are underexplored
No regression or advanced statistical treatment to quantify correlation strength
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All strands fall within the 4â6 band. Research is good but partially relevant, analysis is clear but only partially supported, and evaluation is present but not deeply critical. Performance justifies a mid-band score of 5 out of 12.

strengths: Overall structure follows IB conventions (title page, TOC, clear section headings)
Title and word count are provided, aiding navigability
weaknesses: Page numbering and figure/table numbering are inconsistent
Layout considerations (units on tables, axis labels, error bars, decimal formatting) are uneven or missing
Figures (e.g., distillation setup) contain illegible symbols and artifacts
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure meets the lower end of the 3â4 band, but significant layout and formatting lapses place this in the 1â2 band. An overall score of 2 out of 4 accurately reflects these mixed strengths and weaknesses.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay follows the IB EE structural conventionsâtitle page, abstract omitted (post-2022), table of contents, introduction, methodology, results, discussion, bibliography and appendix. **Sections are sometimes mislabeled or merged, and pagination/figure numbering is inconsistent, but overall structure remains appropriate and navigable**, fulfilling the descriptors for the 3-4 band at a modest level.

The student clearly states the broad theme of solvent concentration and nail-polish removal in the title, introduction, and research question. Background theory on acetone and ethanol is provided and linked to the practical aim of finding a safer remover. **Purpose and scope remain visible throughout the EE**, and the material is largely free of ambiguities. Minor digressions (e.g. lengthy health-impact paragraphs) slightly reduce focus, but overall the topic is communicated accurately and coherently, meeting the descriptors for the upper end of the 3-4 band.

The student processes data (means, standard deviations, error bars) and identifies a positive trend. Explanations are provided but remain **surface-level (e.g., higher concentration â better penetration) without quantitative chemical justification**; confounding factors such as drying time are noted but not analysed numerically. Conclusions are therefore partially supported, fitting the lower range of the 4-6 band.

Primary data are collected across five concentrations with five replicates, uncertainties are calculated and a relevant graph plotted. **Nevertheless, the chosen measurement (mass change of artificial nails) yields values at the limits of balance resolution for four of the five concentrations, so evidence only partially addresses the question.** This constitutes âadequate to goodâ research, thus the bottom of the 4-6 band.

The student displays sound basic knowledge of polarity, hydrogen bonding, distillation and nail anatomy. Calculations for dilution, uncertainty and statistics are correct. **Explanations of why mass change reflects polish removal are thin, and misconceptions appear (e.g. assuming diuretic action applies topically).** Use of literature to deepen chemical reasoning is patchy, giving clear but only partially effective knowledge applicationâtypical of the 3-4 band.

Throughout the EE the student employs chemical vocabularyââsimple distillationâ, âhydrogen bondingâ, âstandard deviationâ, âintermolecular forcesâ, âvolatileââaccurately and consistently. Occasional stylistic lapses (e.g. âOuglitative dataâ) do not impede technical clarity. This sustained, mostly correct terminology meets the upper end of the 3-4 band.

The student outlines a three-part procedure, lists variables, apparatus, risk assessment and error treatment, showing **reasonable but not complete methodological planning**. Key weaknessesâsmall sample size, unverified purity of distillate, questionable mass-change sensitivityâlimit evidence of effective selection. Source use for method justification is minimal. This aligns with âmostly complete methodologyâ but lacking the rigour of the top band, meriting a low 3-4 score.

The student includes a table of contents, headings, figures and tables. A word count and title are supplied. **Page numbers are not clearly shown in the extract, some figures are low resolution, and citation formatting is uneven.** These lapses indicate âsome layout considerations missing or incorrectâ, placing the work in the 1-2 band at the top score of 2.

A reflective section discusses weaknesses (drying-time inconsistency, volumetric accuracy) and proposes improvements and an extension using fractional distillation. **Evaluation is present but not deeply critical; reliability claims conflict with acknowledged limitations, and alternative explanations are not explored.** This corresponds to a âgood discussion with partially critical evaluationâ, warranting a 4 in the 4-6 band.

Citations are provided for background chemistry, nail physiology and safety data, and are referenced in-text. **Most sources are relevant but mainly serve descriptive background; few are integrated into data interpretation or evaluation.** Consequently, application is âmostly relevantâ rather than clearly embedded, matching the middle of the 3-4 band.

The research question is explicitly worded and appears on page 7; it is focused on the **effect of dilution of an acetoneâalcohol distillate on nail-polish removal**. However, the investigation assumes the distillate contains only two components and does not verify composition, meaning the practical work does not fully match the conceptual focus. Connection to later discussion is present but not always tight. These shortcomings place the work solidly in the 3-4 band but closer to the midpoint.

annotations.comment: The introduction effectively contextualizes the topic and clearly states the aim of identifying a safer solvent concentration for nail polish removal.
The title is concise and directly conveys the focus of the investigation, fulfilling the requirement for an informative and engaging title at the start of the report.
Page numbers in the table of contents appear misaligned in places and some entries lack dot leaders. Ensure consistent formatting of section entries and page references.

The background includes an unsupported extrapolation of alcoholâs diuretic effect to topical dehydration. Replace this with evidence on transcutaneous water loss due to solvents to strengthen chemical reasoning.
The structural formula of acetone in Figure 1 clearly illustrates polar and non-polar regions, enhancing the explanation of solvation interactions.
When discussing hydrogen bonding of ethanol, quantify its polarity relative to acetone (e.g., dielectric constant) to provide deeper chemical justification.
The research question appears under a dedicated heading and aligns with the investigationâs aim, reinforcing focus and coherence.

The research question is clearly articulated but could be more succinct. Consider rephrasing to specify independent and dependent variables, for example: âHow does the dilution ratio of a distilled acetoneâethanol mixture affect the percentage of nail polish removed from artificial nails?â
Word count is provided at exactly 4000 words, meeting formatting requirements. Ensure all sections remain within the 4000-word limit, excluding tables, figures and references.
The table of contents lists all major sections and sub-sections, enhancing navigability and demonstrating adherence to structural conventions.
The three-part experimental procedure is clearly outlined and supported by ethical and environmental considerations, demonstrating thoughtful methodological planning.
Assuming the distillate contains only acetone and alcohol is an oversimplification. Discuss potential residual components (e.g., oils, denaturants) and their effect on nail-polish solubility.

The independent variable table applies CâVâ=CâVâ without verifying initial distillate purity. Consider validating composition (e.g., GC analysis) before assuming 100% acetoneâalcohol content.
Controlled variable descriptions for distillation temperature are procedural rather than quantitative. Specify acceptable temperature range and calibration protocol to tighten methodological control.
The decision to use artificial nails to avoid human-subject variability is well justified and enhances consistency of results.
Processed data table omits units for standard deviation and percent change columns. Include units (g, %) to avoid ambiguity.
The graph effectively visualizes the positive trend between solvent concentration and polish removal, supporting the investigationâs conclusions.
Graph axes lack units and error bars are not plotted. Add axis labels (%, g) and error bars to reflect data variability and improve clarity.
The bibliography mixes citation styles and omits publisher details for some sources. Choose a single style (e.g., APA or Chicago) and ensure completeness.
Appendix calculations (pages 29â30) repeat examples already shown in the body. Remove redundancies and focus the main text on essential derivations.
Calibration details for the analytical scale (Â±0.001 g) are missing. Include frequency and method of calibration to support accuracy and reproducibility of mass measurements.
Figure 3 depicting the distillation setup contains illegible symbols and table artifacts. Ensure the diagram is clear, properly labeled, and corresponds to described apparatus.
The description of dilutions via CâVâ=CâVâ is correct, but an explicit example calculation would help readers unfamiliar with the formula follow the procedure.
Qualitative observations are recorded for each experimental part, adding valuable context to quantitative results and supporting deeper evaluation.
Observations note clarity and odor but omit potential azeotrope behavior during distillation. Consider discussing boiling-point plateau as evidence of azeotrope formation.
Raw data tables inconsistently use commas and periods for decimals, which may confuse international readers. Standardize decimal notation throughout.
The standard deviation formula uses a population denominator; with five replicates, using nâ1 (sample standard deviation) would more accurately represent experimental uncertainty.
Consider performing a regression analysis (e.g., linear or nonlinear fit) to quantify correlation strength (RÂ²) and discuss its significance for critical evaluation.
The conclusion accurately summarizes key findings but overstates data reliability. Reconcile the impact of drying-time inconsistencies with claims of methodological consistency.
Evaluation identifies drying-time as a major challenge and proposes a viable improvement to standardize it. This analytical reflection demonstrates good engagement with the experimental process.
The appendix tables are extensive and may distract from the main report. Consider summarizing key tables in the body and moving full raw data to supplementary materials.

similarExemplars.title: To what extent does the concentration of ethanol (0-50%) affect the rate of hydrolysis of aspirin, as determined via visible spectrophotometry. Furthermore, investigate the effect of molecular structure in terms of the number and position of hydroxyl groups of 3 carbon chain alcohols (propan-1-ol, propan-2ol, propylene glycol and glycerol) at 50% concentration on the above.

userSubmission.researchQuestion: To what extent does the concentration of ethanol (0-50%) affect the rate of hydrolysis of aspirin, as determined via visible spectrophotometry. Furthermore, investigate the effect of molecular structure in terms of the number and position of hydroxyl groups of 3 carbon chain alcohols (propan-1-ol, propan-2ol, propylene glycol and glycerol) at 50% concentration on the above.
